title: Very limited database
text: Out of the 20 cookbooks I have and use, only 3 were available in the indexed library. The rest were either not indexed or unrecognized.

title: Cooking with myself.
text: Rekindled passion for my beloved cookbooks. Slow down, chop, breathe…♥️📚

title: decent, but could go further for the price
text: I don't expect full recipes here, but I do wish there was more flexibility in the app. Tagging books by a category we choose would help a lot, especially for filtering. (Such as, I have a few books that are more challenging "project" cooking - so I'd love to be able to tag a book so it doesn't show up when I want a weeknight meal.) I wish there were other view options than one long list of recipe titles - like small thumbnails or something. Obviously it would be great to sort by cook time or difficulty if applicable, but I understand that would be very hard to index. The other big thing, which I don't think would be possible, is to get the book photos instead of user-submitted photos for the recipes. Almost none have photos, and those that do aren't appetizing, making me not want to cook it. I really appreciate what the app does, and the amount it DID index was good (not great for more obscure or vintage books, which I understand.) But it's not functional enough for me to subscribe yet.

title: Terrific App!
text: The CookShelf App has made my cookbook collection so much more useable! Being able to search by ingredients is a game-changer and I also find it very helpful to quickly search for specific recipes from specific books. I love being able to save notes, ratings and changes to recipes I’ve cooked, as well—all in the App. I have rediscovered some real gems on my own bookshelves—Thank you!

title: I didn’t expect to like this, but it’s great!
text: From my brief use of the Cookshelf app, I can tell that it will be really helpful to me.  I’m not great at Meal Planning, but I think I can manage it better using Cookshelf.  I also love that you can check the ingredients of a recipe while you are at the grocery store, just in case you forgot something or want to pursue a new idea. It’s a great companion to Eat Your Books, which I love,love,love. I think it may take a few days to get Cookshelf figured out, but I look forward to exploring what Cookshelf can do.

title: Essential for cookbook lovers
text: As an avid cook with a cookbook collection of nearly 400, this app is a complete game changer. I can search every recipe in my collection by ingredient!

title: Doesn’t have a big selection
text: I love the idea but most of my cookbooks are not in their data base so you can request to have it indexed or index it yourself . But first you need to read the indexing module. A lot of work but worth it in the end.

title: Traveling Kitchen
text: I’m liking this app so far. Especially when I’m at the grocery store & i want to meal plan/meal prep for the night or the week. It is a little disappointing that the ingredients list don’t give to the measurements from the book. But no big deal. It is also handy that it gives you the page number for the recipe so you can go home & pop open the book when ready to start cooking. Now, the only reason for the 4 star rate is because the app had missing ingredients on the list. I made a peanut butter cake from an old Betty Crocker cookbook that my grandma passed down to me, & it was missing items on list. Thankfully it was a recipe I made before discovering this app. Otherwise, a trip back to the store would have been a pain.

title: Love this app
text: I collect cookbooks. Anytime I have a new hobby I must find the cookbook to go with it. This app has changed up how I do dinner as before I had so many cookbooks I started to favor some over the others. Now I search whatever meat I have and try new recipes from books I hadn't opened in awhile or really got to test out.

title: Very disappointed
text: I was so looking forward to upload my favorite books and use the recipes from my phone or tablet. There are no measurements/ingredient amounts showing in the recipes and it admittedly could leave out ingredients. The recipes direct you back to the cookbook. With upgrades, this is a great idea but without the full recipes, it’s not practical at all.

title: Unsure of usage
text: The app asked me for books, then seemed to take a long time to “index” the books I entered. None of them actually ended up getting indexed, and since the app claimed it wouldn’t give recipes, I’m kind of unsure what the app is supposed to do. I enter ingredients and it tells me which cook book to go to? I suppose that could be useful, but so could just flipping to my popular bookmarked pages and buying the ingredients a day ahead.

title: Saved me a another shopping trip
text: I like to plan out my meals for the week and then shop for what I need for those meals. Today at the grocery store, I couldn't find an ingredient for a salad I had planned. But with CookShelf on my phone, I was able to look up recipes using what was available. The whole recipe wasn’t there, but the ingredients I needed to make it were listed. I didn’t have to go home, find another recipe for that night, and make another shopping trip. Yeah!

title: One of a kind great
text: I have so many cookbooks and this is just an amazing tool for getting more use out of them! I love seeing what other people are making from the books I have, and reading their comments. I can search for an ingredient I have and see what recipes I can make. I think the cost is very justified when you consider the work that goes into indexing.

title: Cookbooks Not Indexed
text:
I was so excited to start scanning my cookbooks but the ones I scanned were not indexed yet. Making the app a bit pointless. 
If I am paying for the app I was hoping that my cookbooks would be ready to go.

title: Brilliant but misses it
text: I was knocked out by how easy it was to scan and see my cookbook library appear on the app. Then brokenhearted to realize that it doesn’t have the recipe to read & follow, just directs you to that recipe. You still gotta pull out the book, mess with propping it open. I kind of love reading & doing recipes off my tablet, so this is a dealbreaker for me😢

title: My Dreams Came True!
text: As a self proclaimed cookbook fanatic this app is a must! I’m so glad this came to fruition!!!

title: Best app I have
text: I love collecting cookbooks snd recipes, and I finally have a way to search across all of them. It’s like my cookbooks merged with online searching because of this app.  It’s so fun to scroll through everything in one step. I’ve started cooking more since using the app.  Highly recommend.

title: Needs work, steep cost for what it is
text: I saw this advertised on social media and downloaded it immediately—what an awesome idea!! However, I was disappointed to realize you have a 5 book limit for the free version… why would I even pay monthly for the premium? For my own cookbooks? For indexing? The app doesn’t even include the recipes, the UI isn’t great, and the fact that there’s a monthly cost is enraging. I’d rather pay $10 or even $15 upfront if I had to as opposed to $40/year forever. Ended up deleting because I can’t add all my cookbooks and feels ridiculous to pay for it.

title: no actual recipes in app?
text: I am so confused by this app. I scanned 3 cookbooks, and I don’t see any recipes, only an ingredients list. Makes it quite pointless, no? I’ll be canceling before my free trial is up.

title: Super useful
text: My cookbook collection keeps growing. I used to look at the index of one book after another when I needed a particular recipe.  I might need this app once a month, but it’s still worth the money. Sometimes I just browse random ingredients for fun.

title: Brilliant app
text: CookShelf is fabulous. My favorite feature is the ingredient list that it gives for recipes I am searching. That helps immensely with determining whether I can make something-do I have the ingredients or pantry staples or will it require a trip to the market? Worth the subscription.

title: Great idea
text: This is a great idea and I’ve wanted a clearinghouse for my cookbooks. The ingredients lists could be a bit more thorough and I wish that the folks at Mission Control could update indexes more frequently .

title: Great concept, but not there yet
text:
This is a great concept for an app and I want to love it - BUT, I’m guessing because the app is relatively new, only one of my cookbooks is actually indexed (out of the 10 I’ve added to my shelf thus far). I’ve requested indexing for each one, and will update my review with how long it takes for them to be added to the database.  

I’m not super inclined to pay so much for an app that is clearly still so underdeveloped and doesn’t have the capacity to actually do what it says it will do without waiting for them to do updates - so, unless they’re crazy responsive with the indexing, I will probably cancel my trial - though, I may check back at a later date.

title: Love It!
text: This is a game changer! Have a bunch of cannellini beans on hand? No problem. Scroll through every recipe for them in every cookbook you own. Love that the app quickly shows the other ingredients needed, and gives you the page number to reference. Lovely. ❤️

title: Really cool idea
text: Love the concept. But at 40 a year it’s just too much for me. Was a neat app and it was really easy to use!

title: Almost Perfect
text: I have the free version with my top 5 cookbooks loaded. I love being able to quickly find recipes, but even better is quickly searching by ingredient. For example, right now I have an open jar of chilies in adobo sauce to use up. I was able to instantly find quite a few recipes from my collection that use that ingredient. Perfect if you’re at home and trying to plan meals. This isn’t 5 stars for me yet because it doesn’t list quantities. So if I’m looking up a recipe while at the store or away from my cookbooks, I had to guess how much to buy. I’m sure there is licensing reasons for this, but it’s still something to be aware of. Also, I’ll add more cookbooks if the Pro version pricing comes down. Right now it’s too expensive to justify.

title: Didn’t know how much I needed this
text: Genius idea!!! I never pay for apps and I got this subscription immediately.

title: Not as advertised
text: It scanned the ISBN’s of my cookbooks but most needed to be indexed into the app and those that were there, tapped on a recipe and it would show me the ingredients but no directions

title: No device sync
text: Doesn’t sync across devices. Pretty disappointing as I use iPad for a lot of cooking

title: Be sure you want this before starting the free trial!
text: This app is a good idea, but it just didn’t work for me.  Extremely disappointed that it auto renewed, and despite my cancelling it as soon as I saw the charge, I still have the privilege of paying $40 for the next year for an app I do not use.    Should I have put it on my calendar? Of course. But life is busy, and I’m disappointed that I can’t even get a prorated refund less than a week after it renewed itself.

title: Great idea, but…
text: Great idea and concept. I downloaded the app and tried scanning several of my cookbooks, you’re limited to 5 until you subscribe.  Anyhow I tried 10 of my cookbooks and only 1 was found. Uninstalled…..maybe down the road I’ll try again……but I doubt it.

title: No more hunting for recipes
text: I wanted a way to catalog all my cookbooks so I could quickly locate recipes, especially ones I’d used previously. So far I’ve scanned over 280 books from my collection (everyone needs a hobby right?) and the app has shown the value already.

title: Boo subscriptions
text: I’d buy it for a one time price - like a cookbook - but never on a subscription.

title: I love this app!!
text: I absolutely love cookbooks. Buts it’s been really hard to use mine because it’s been impossible to search across my collection and know what good and what not. Until now! This app genuinely makes it a joy to collect cookbooks. I can digitally search for recipes across my collections. Read recipe reviews. It’s an absolute must for cookbook enthusiasts. Now I feel like I can expand my collection guilt free! Because I will use them all!

title: Just ok
text: The app does in fact log your books and tracks the recipes. It however does not allow you to take the recipe with you and give you the step by step or quantities. A little misleading. It will give you an ingredient list but that does not always guarantee they are correct. I’ve found a few in the short time I used the free trial that were incorrect. If you added the full recipes to the page it would be worth much more. But this stage is definitely not worth paying for.

title: Game changer for actually using your cookbooks
text: I love being able to search my own cookbooks for things to make rather than googling! It’s nice to be able to just look up an ingredient or dish in the app and know which of my cookbooks have the recipes I need.

title: This price is hard to swallow…
text: It’s obvious from even a small amount of use that this is an amazing resource, but OMG I’m struggling to accept the cost. I’m excited to use it with a first month free deal I got, but imagining keeping it long term makes me grit my teeth because of the cost. Hope they change the way they do things. It’s a wonderful resource, but many home cooks (who often cook partly to save money) will never find out because of the ongoing $$$ drain. Say I love it and want to use it as my personal cooking library index —- in 10 years I’ll have spent $400… and that’s if the price stays the same…

title: Almost perfect
text: Love it but still would like to be able to filter recipes according to ingredients like on Eat your Books!

title: Great in theory, not in Application
text: I’ve been waiting for an app like this for a very long time. And when I got done scanning more than 100 cookbooks, I found out that many of them aren’t indexed or only have one or two recipes indexed. So, a waste of an afternoon and a waste of money.

title: My dream all!!
text: I have over 120 cookbooks so this is something I have been needing for years!!! I will use this everyday and it has already changed the way I cook.  If you love being the kitchen, you need this app.

title: Anyone with more than 10 cookbooks has thought of this app concept
text: But I’m glad someone actually made it. I like that it both directs you to the recipe in the book itself but if it is online, will give you the link.

title: Great Resource
text: Love this app — thanks!

title: How did I not think of this??
text: I could not like this app more. I strive to make more recipes from my cookbooks but often end up searching online for ideas from outside the market. This app helps me find the recipes I know I’ll like.

title: Please consider one-time payment
text:
I would absolutely love to pay for this app as if I were paying for an incredible cookbook: one time. I don’t want to spend the time uploading all my cookbooks and getting organized digitally if there’s a risk the cost of 
a subscription would go up each year, or with the feeling that I’m paying $40 every year for the rest of my life (easily $1600+) just to browse my own books.

title: Why a subscription?
text: I love the idea of this app and I’m sure if I subscribed and got to add more than five books I would genuinely love it. I’m so over subscriptions draining my bank account when they aren’t necessary. I would gladly pay a lump sum for this app even if it were a bit pricey. But having a subscription that’s a bit pricey is just not something I’m willing to do anymore.

title: Must-have app for cooking enthusiasts
text: Enormously helpful app to catalogue your cookbooks and recipes. The app has an impressive database of published cookbooks, including some rare editions. I’m so thankful that they migrated from a website to an app—the barcode scanner has made my life so much easier!

title: Game Changer!
text: Ok, I got a 30 day trial (shoutout Anchovy Book Co!) and thought it would be useless (hello I already have all the cookbooks in print, what do I need an app?) and I was SO WRONG. This is amazing. It’s like searching a recipe app but if your own cookbooks. Sure, you could flip though a bunch of books and find a recipe you want to make but why do that when you can search the app for “ground turkey” and it will pull up every recipe from your cookbooks that use that ingredient. Love the comments feature too!

title: The app still needs some work.
text: I don’t find the app nearly as useful as the eatyourbooks.com website. I think the two column graphical display of search results is a lot harder to use than the single column text display on the site. It requires a lot more eye movement. In addition, It lists only the recipe name and the cookbook name, no ingredients. You have to click through to see those. The filter for results is also lacking. There doesn’t appear to be any way to limit the search to say course or ethnicity. I’ll check back from time to time to see how things go.

title: No recipes
text: The ingredients are listed but not recipes. I guess this is a copyright issue that publishers blocked. Am I right?

title: Barcode scanner not great
text: So far I’ve had the app 10 minutes and gone through 2 shelves of books. It’s not able to scan about half. Books like Tartine, and How to Cook Everything, not obscure cookbooks at all.

title: Please display qty of ingredients
text:
I really enjoy this app. I don’t find myself using it long term due to the lack of qty not displaying on the ingredients required for the recipe. 

If I am at a farmers market and see ready to eat peaches, I would like to know how much to buy to make a recipe that I have in a book at home. Unfortunately I would have to leave and come back to buy or guess the qty. 

Please update! This will be a game changer!

title: Disappointing release
text: I waited so long for eat your books to release an app and was thrilled when they finally announced the release. I wanted to be able to easily leave recipe comments & upload photos.  I was hoping it would encourage other users to leave their comments & photos as well, as that is where so much of the value of my subscription lies. Unfortunately this app doesn’t allow photo uploads for recipes. I still need to upload those from the website.

title: Love your cookbooks again (or even more!)
text:
Have you’ve ever bough a specific ingredient for a single recipe and now wonder what the heck to do with the rest of it? This app if for you! I no longer feel guilty about buying pomegranate molasses, kaffir lime leaves, or harissa paste.  

I have a major cookbook problem and CookShelf has made my addiction manageable. I love that I can search across all of my books for a single ingredient and see reviews of recipes from other cooks. It’s helped me rediscover lost loves and find new ones.

title: Instant gratification
text: I’ve been using Eat Your Books for years to search my cookbooks but the website is very slow and you have to log in every time you use it.  This app gives me the same information but is much faster.

title: Fav kitchen companion
text: Love the handy digital access to my favorite cookbooks - and the reverse search where I can find recipes that match the contents of my fridge!

title: Game changer
text: So easy to add your cookbooks by scanning the bar codes and whenever you want to make something but can’t remember what recipes are where you just list an ingredient you have and search all your cookbooks to find recipes. It’s so handy; I love it!

title: Worth the wait!
text: Since I started collecting cookbooks I have loved the idea of EYB & maintained a free membership. I knew I wouldn’t use a website enough to justify a payed membership. Apps are just my go to way of doing things now. I was anxiously awaiting this app that I heard they were working on. I am happy to say that after just a few days of using the free trial I know that I will be happy to pay for a membership. I know with this app I will be able to utilize my cookbook collection so much better. My only criticism of the system in general is I wish there was a way to eliminate multiple entries of the same book existing in the database. That way all comments & reviews on that particular book could be under one book & not spread across multiple entries.

title: Great kitchen tool
text: It helps me use my treasure trove of cookbooks instead of scrolling through dubious recipe blogs, dodging popup ads!

title: Loved the Website and App is Great!
text: Eatyourbooks was a game changer for my kitchen and having the app makes everything even easier! So thrilled they came out with this!

title: Eat my produce!
text: This app is a game changer. I can put in my veggies on hand and instantly find great recipes from all my cookbooks without having to look through each index.

title: My favorite new app
text:
Incredibly helpful! I’m halfway around the world from my cookbooks and still using them to plan a meal when I get home.

Looking forward to using this for years to come. Great work team.

title: My Go-To Kitchen App!
text: Cookshelf is such a game-changer for organizing recipes! I love how easy it is to save, search, and plan meals all in one place. The design is clean, intuitive, and makes cooking so much more enjoyable. Highly recommend for anyone who loves to cook, or just wants dinner planning to be less stressful!

title: Great app!
text: This app is a joy to use! So easy to add my cookbooks and great when I’m at the store and need to check the ingredients I need for a recipe.

title: Misleading!! You have to pay for it
text:
Nowhere does it mention that you have to pay for this app until it asks you to create an account.
Deleted my account immediately.

title: Limited use
text:
I tried this and most of my recipe books weren’t in the index. There’s also no category browsing, like any standard table of contents, it’s just search by ingredient.

Also, the app only contains the ingredients, it doesn’t include the method; now, there may well be good copyright reasons for that, but the point is if you think this app will replace your recipe books in the kitchen, it won’t. It’s just a search engine for some of your books.

title: Fantastic app for home chefs
text: This is an amazing app and has finally helped me organise my huge cookbook collection. Now I’m making meals I wouldn’t have necessarily noticed before, and I love that they’re adding new features like shopping lists and meal plans. I hope one of the features they can add one day is custom books or foreign books!

title: Rent your own books.
text: Let me get this straight - you would like me to rent my books from you. Books I already own. Um…no thanks, I have already parted with the money for these.

title: Great idea but poor execution
text: I downloaded the app after seeing so many ads for it and it sounded so appealing. Unfortunately the book I have downloaded (rather popular ones) were not referenced yet and therefore the app is useless

title: Great idea but library lacking
text: Downloaded this app after seeing it advertised, what a great idea! Sadly a lot of my books weren’t on the app at all, and many of the ones that were had not been indexed (which entirely defeats the point of the app, isn’t the point to pay for the index???). After 5 scans it prompted me to subscribe, but having had almost half my collection missing from the app there’s no incentive for me to subscribe. For any value for money the library needs to be extended much wider - many of my books are very popular books. For now I’ve loaded the 5 books I rarely look at and will see how I get on with those

title: Love this app
text:
If you have a bit of a cookery book addiction this is the app for you.  It’s brilliant for finding recipes even from some older, more obscure books and means you get to know and use your books rather than ending up googling second rate recipes.  I’ve been using it all the time since I downloaded it.  It’s
like receiving an exciting new cookery book in the post every day and actually using it.

title: Be careful!
text:
⚠️ MISLEADING – READ BEFORE DOWNLOADING
Do not be fooled by the description. Nowhere does it mention that this app requires payment to use – you only find out after you’ve signed up and created an account. By that point, you’re already locked in, and to make matters worse, there is no way to delete your account.
This is completely unacceptable. The pricing should be made clear upfront, before anyone hands over their personal details. Consider yourself warned – avoid this app until they are transparent about their costs and give users the ability to remove their data.

title: Love this
text: A fabulous app, a way to organise the recipes I want to cook from my books and track what I’ve cooked with a self review. I usually don’t pay for apps but I couldn’t not have this. With so many cookbooks and my eye on many more this is invaluable.

title: So glad I found this app
text: I've long struggled with a way to effectively search my cookery book collection, so this is a game changer. It’s really easy to use, especially with the barcode scanner - amazed that even my most obscure titles are indexed. I’m already planning next week's menu using books that I wouldn't normally consider. And justification (not that I need it) for the shelf space!

title: Easy to use
text:
It’s quite easy to use, and simple- which I like. It was based from the EatYourBooks website. On the site , you can view a list of recipes in a potential book , eg on kindle,  before buying it. Unfortunately, the app doesn’t have this function.  

 Perhaps this can be incorporated?

It’s much faster than the website too 👍🏼

title: Take my ******* money now!
text:
I LOATHE subscription apps but I’m going make an exception for this one if it goes like I think it’s going to. I invented this app YEARS ago, unfortunately I’m not an app developer so it just stayed in my head until it popped up on my instagram feed yesterday. I’ve spent a couple of hours scanning my cookery books ( 88 ) a few had to be searched for by name rather than scanning but I suspect that’s my phone problem and not app and even so it was super easy to do. I only had a couple of books not listed and they were like Marks and Spencer’s or freebies. I was surprised so many of them were there as some were old and very random. A few don’t have the recipes indexed yet so I’ve requested that, have no idea if it will work but it won’t matter anyway in the long run.
I’ve already committed to making 50 recipes ( you’d think someone with 88 cookbooks could manage more but here we are ) and have high hopes that my cookery book reading will actually turn into cooking. 
A suggestion for the app ( and it might already be there and I’ve just not found ) would be able to tag books somehow. I have favourites in some places and the not so popular kids are hidden away a bit would be lovely to be instantly see where I’ve stashed them but that’s just me getting excited.

Thank you for making this app! ❤️

title: Q about subscription
text: Hi! Will I loose access to my books library if I don’t renew the annual subscription?

title: Excellent
text:
I’ve been using eat your books for years and am a huge fan. The app, in its first iteration, is lovely and visual and makes it easy to search through my cookbooks. 

I use the shopping list extensively for meal planning so hope they add that soon in an update :)

title: We love this app
text: This apps unlocks the power of our 90 odd cookbooks.  We love how we can search by ingredients.  No more flipping through indexes or leafing through cookbooks with no indexes.  We have started using most of our cookbooks again.  We wish it was offered at a more affordable price point.  We think it is overpriced; however now that we have it we definitely use it multiple times every week.

title: Great Idea But…
text: I really like this concept but unfortunately it doesn’t deliver on search functionality. Only about half my recipe books could be found. Of those, that it did find, I tested searching for recipes I know are in my books, but it did not return any results even when being very specific or more vague on ingredients. Too bad.

title: Seems like a great idea but
text: The cost is way to high, I already own the books so why would I pay a monthly cost to get access to books I already have. A one time fee sure but a subscription na

title: A treasure for the cook with too many cookbooks
text:
This app fills a unique niche: it’s for the cook who owns too many cookbooks. It makes it easy to search through your personal collection of physical and digital cookbooks looking for recipes, ideas about using ingredients and the like. If you just have a handful of cookbooks, you don’t need it. But if you’ve bought a number of cookbooks over the years and you’ve added them to the Eat your Books site, this app makes it really easy to search.
Is it perfect? No. But I don’t know of anything else like it. And it’s so useful. 
Yes, the app costs money. Programming takes time. Updating programs like this one takes time. But think of it this way: For a relatively small cost, you can restore the usefulness of those cookbooks that you bought over the years. You’ll save yourself time. Rediscoveries await.

title: Love it! But…
text: I really love this concept. And it’s a great looking app. Super intuitive. The cost though…woof. I would pay a flat fee, but this cost ongoing, it’s a bit much. Hopefully this will change in the future!

title: Sneaky
text: Asks for a bunch of info then hits you with the subscription fee. I can’t stand how everything is on a subscription model now, instead of letting you just buy it once and use it forever (I don’t mind paying for updates when I’m ready to upgrade but don’t want to pay monthly forever). And to not be upfront about the cost until after getting a bunch of information out of the user doesn’t sit well with me.

title: Bait and switch
text: Deleted it as soon as I opened it. Why not be honest and upfront and list that it costs $50/ year to basically index recipe TITLES? So let’s be clear, not the actual recipe, you still need to pull out your recipe book. And for that an annual sub costs the equivalent of a hardcover recipe book. I can see paying a few dollars once to purchase it, but an ongoing sub? Ridiculous

title: Meh
text: Cool concept. Search your cookbooks based on title or ingredients. But still need to get out your cookbook for quantities and method. Personally wouldn’t pay a $60 annual subscription for this service. Probably would pay a smaller one off price though.

title: Great idea, massively overpriced
text: It’s a cool idea, I would use my books more and would absolutely pay a small convenience fee. But an ongoing $60 subscription is way too much for searching content.

title: Amazing way to use your books
text: Such a great way of using all those books on the shelf which you never look at. Also good to search for ideas to use food in the fridge. Highly recommend.

title: AMAZING!!
text: Finally!!! My friends and I have always talked about how much we would like to see all our cookbook and magazine recipes in one place, and this app does it! It’s so quick to setup and so intuitive to use. I am so happy to have this in my cooking toolbox now 😊

title: Love love love this app
text:
Finally an app that translates to less time on phone, more time in real life!
This was advertised to me a few weeks back. I did a trial, fell in love, and am using it daily. Happy to pay for something that makes using my cookbook trove actually enjoyable, not a chore! I’ve collected thousands of dollars worth of cookbooks and have spent years looking for recipes online instead, so that I don’t have to go through every book. 
Thank you thank you thank you for creating such a useful app! Am recommending it to everyone!
